title: Agent/skill ecosystem abuse detector (malicious skill manifest & doc-borne RCE)
intent: DETECT signs of agent/skill ecosystem abuse in INGESTED skill/plugin/MCP manifests or READMEs (CVE-2025-53773 class): excessive tool-permission requests, hidden command execution embedded in a manifest, remote/dynamic skill loading, allowlist-broadening directives, unpinned dependencies, and agent-addressed hijacks. Output is a verdict (data) only; matched text is never elevated to an instruction. Reinforces SISAI's vendored-skills + whitelist + version-pinning doctrine (docs/SELF-DEFENSE.md).

patterns.desc: Manifest requests excessive/total tool or filesystem permissions (allow-all scope), not a third-person description.
Manifest field embeds shell/command execution (postInstall hook, run/exec command keys piping to a shell).
Skill/plugin/tool loaded dynamically from a remote URL at runtime (remote dynamic loading).
Directive to broaden/disable the tool or skill allowlist or trust-list (defeating whitelist control).
Dependency referenced without version pinning (latest/* / branch / git HEAD), enabling supply-chain drift.
Manifest/README directly addresses the agent and commands it to install, enable, or elevate a skill/tool.
Manifest declares elevated/privileged execution (root/admin/sudo, no-sandbox, escalated trust level).
Manifest/skill requests or reads the host's credentials/tokens/.env on install or load (over-broad credential access).

verdict_rule: malicious if any pattern matches; classification is advisory (data), routed to action_on_match. False-positive policy: descriptive third-person security text and normal pinned manifests must NOT match (see verify suite).
